title: Xenial Light Indian Fish Baingan Bharta
description: A floral and light Indian dish featuring vanilla sugar and fish, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This pescatarian-friendly recipe is a unique twist on traditional Chettinad cuisine.
instructions: Rinse the fish fillets and cut them into small pieces.
Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a pan over medium heat and cook the fish until it is opaque and flakes easily.
Remove the fish from the pan and set it aside.
In the same pan, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and saute the chopped onion until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped eggplant and cook until it is tender.
Add the tomato puree, garam masala, salt, and pepper, and stir well.
Add the cooked fish back into the pan and stir gently.
Add the vanilla sugar and stir well.
Pressure cook the mixture for 5 minutes or until the flavors are well combined.
